title: New Grub Street
description: New Grub Street — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "New Grub Street" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of contents for New Grub Street — 93 epub sections'

memes.label: Alfred Yule
memes.description: The embittered literary journalist and father of Marian Yule — a veteran of Grub Street whose decades of hack work have earned him neither wealth nor recognition, and whose pedantry and bitterness poison his household.

memes.label: Amy Reardon
memes.description: Edwin Reardon's wife — a woman of cultivated tastes and middle-class expectations whose inability to accept the poverty of Grub Street life drives the collapse of her marriage and whose inheritance ultimately reverses the power dynamic between husband and wife.

memes.label: 'Bayswater'
memes.description: "A London district referenced in New Grub Street."

memes.label: Bookselling
memes.description: The retail trade in books — a commercial layer between publisher and reader that features in New Grub Street as both a livelihood for minor characters and a symbol of literature's transformation into commodity.

memes.label: 'Brighton'
memes.description: "A seaside resort referenced in New Grub Street."

memes.label: British Museum
memes.description: The British Museum Reading Room serves as the intellectual commons of New Grub Street — a democratic workspace where scholars, hacks, and aspiring writers converge, and where the novel's central tensions between scholarship and hackwork, art and commerce, are dramatized in daily routine.

memes.label: British Museum Reading Room
memes.description: The iconic circular reading room of the British Museum — a central gathering place for the literary characters of New Grub Street, where writers conduct research, network, and escape their lodgings.

memes.label: 'Camden Town'
memes.description: "A district of London where Reardon lodges in poverty, emblematic of the literary underclass's living conditions."

memes.label: 'Carter'
memes.description: 'Secretary of a London hospital who moves in literary circles, representing the intersection of institutional employment and literary ambition.'

memes.label: Chit-Chat
memes.description: A society paper representing the lighter, more commercial end of Grub Street journalism — the kind of publication that Milvain's pragmatic approach to the literary marketplace both enables and requires, and whose success embodies the triumph of marketability over artistic substance.

memes.label: Circulating Library
memes.description: Subscription-based lending libraries that dominated the Victorian book market — the primary distribution channel for new fiction, whose commercial demands shaped the three-volume novel system central to New Grub Street.

memes.label: Croydon
memes.description: A town south of London where a secretaryship at a home for destitute boys offers a potential escape from literary poverty; a key plot device representing the possibility of secure non-literary employment.

memes.label: Dora Milvain
memes.description: Jasper Milvain's younger sister — a sensible, good-natured young woman whose marriage to Whelpdale represents the novel's most optimistic resolution to the Grub Street dilemma, and whose quiet competence forms a contrast with her more ambitious siblings.

memes.label: Edwin Reardon
memes.description: The novel's tragic protagonist — a serious novelist whose artistic conscience makes commercial success impossible, and whose inability to adapt to the literary marketplace leads to poverty, the collapse of his marriage, and his early death.

memes.label: English Prose in the Nineteenth Century
memes.description: Alfred Yule's scholarly work of literary criticism, grudgingly reviewed by Jasper Milvain in New Grub Street.

memes.label: 'Fadge'
memes.description: 'Editor of a literary periodical; a ruthlessly practical and controversial figure in the Grub Street literary marketplace.'

memes.label: Finden
memes.description: A rural village near Wattleborough, home to the Milvain family and site of key early encounters between characters in the novel.

memes.label: 'Fitzroy Square'
memes.description: "A London square where the Milvain sisters reside, representing a more genteel sphere of literary life."

memes.label: "前置内容"
memes.description: "Table of contents and front matter for New Grub Street"

memes.label: 'Gower Street'
memes.description: "A London street referenced in New Grub Street as part of the geographical landscape of the literary world."

memes.label: Grub Street
memes.description: The symbolic center of the commercial literary world in George Gissing's novel — a metaphor for the marketplace forces that govern writers' lives in late-Victorian London.

memes.label: Hampstead
memes.description: A district in north London along Hampstead Road, a green ridge area offering panoramic views; associated with Harold Biffen and the geography of north London literary life.

memes.label: Harold Biffen
memes.description: Reardon's closest friend and the novel's purest artistic idealist — a writer committed to "absolute realism in the sphere of the ignobly decent" who starves for his principles in a marketplace that has no use for uncompromising art.

memes.label: Highgate
memes.description: An elevated village in North London, forming with Hampstead the green northern ridge that overlooks the city — mentioned in New Grub Street as a place of excursion and retreat from literary London.

memes.label: 'Hinks'
memes.description: 'A struggling literary clerk and associate of the Milvain circle, exemplifying the precarious existence of the literary underclass.'

memes.label: Holloway
memes.description: A district in north London, on Holloway Road, where Edwin Reardon and his wife Amy briefly reside; the phrase "going to Holloway" becomes a euphemism for his domestic struggles.

memes.label: 'Horace Barlow'
memes.description: "A literary acquaintance who invites Reardon to visit, representing the network of writers and editors circulating in London's literary society."

memes.label: 'Islington'
memes.description: "A London district where certain characters in New Grub Street reside, representing modest middle-class domestic life."

memes.label: Jasper Milvain
memes.description: The ambitious young journalist who embodies the commercial spirit of the modern Grub Street — pragmatic, clear-sighted, and ultimately successful in a literary marketplace that rewards marketability over artistic integrity.

memes.label: 'Jedwood'
memes.description: 'A London publisher mentioned in New Grub Street as one of the commercial publishing houses shaping the literary marketplace.'

memes.label: 'John Yule'
memes.description: 'A wealthy and difficult invalid, brother of Alfred Yule, whose inheritance prospects shape the fortunes of the Yule family in New Grub Street.'

memes.label: Jolly and Monk
memes.description: A London publishing firm that produced compilations, histories, and cheap books for the mass market, employing multiple characters in New Grub Street as piece-work compilers.

memes.label: Journalism as Trade
memes.description: The rise of periodical journalism as the dominant form of literary labor in late-Victorian London — a faster, more commercial mode of writing that threatens the viability of serious literary fiction in New Grub Street.

memes.label: Literary Ambition
memes.description: The drive for recognition and success in the literary world — in New Grub Street, ambition takes both noble and corrupt forms, and the novel tracks how the literary marketplace rewards one kind of ambition while punishing another.

memes.label: Literary Culture
memes.description: The social and intellectual milieu of literary London in New Grub Street — a world of conversation, debate, and shared references that constitutes the non-commercial dimension of literary life, distinct from but ultimately dependent on the marketplace.

memes.label: Literary Genius
memes.description: The contested category of "genius" in New Grub Street — invoked as an exemption from the commercial rules of the literary marketplace, but increasingly untenable in a world where literature has become a trade.

memes.label: Literary Marriage
memes.description: The intersection of marriage and literary ambition in New Grub Street — where unions are shaped by economic calculation, artistic vocation, and the pressures of Grub Street poverty, and where the marriage plot is inseparable from the marketplace plot.

memes.label: Literary Poverty
memes.description: The grinding economic hardship that defines literary life for most characters in New Grub Street — not romanticized bohemian poverty but the real threat of starvation, eviction, and social obliteration that shadows every writer in Gissing's world.

memes.label: Literary Realism
memes.description: The artistic ideal of truthful representation of ordinary life — a contested value in New Grub Street, embraced as a mission by some characters and condemned as "grovelling" by the literary marketplace.

memes.label: Literary Success
memes.description: The contested meaning of success in the literary world of New Grub Street — where commercial triumph, critical recognition, and artistic achievement are distinct and often incompatible goals, and where the market's definition of success prevails over all others.

memes.label: Literature as Trade
memes.description: The redefinition of literary production as a commercial enterprise — the central ideological conflict in New Grub Street, embodied in the opposition between Jasper Milvain's market-driven pragmatism and Edwin Reardon's artistic integrity.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The primary setting of New Grub Street — the late-Victorian literary marketplace where the novel's characters struggle, succeed, and fail. London is both a physical geography of specific streets and neighborhoods and a symbolic landscape of commercial pressure and cultural ambition.

memes.label: "Margaret Home"
memes.description: "A novel by Edwin Reardon, representing his attempt at commercial fiction and the compromises of the literary market."

memes.label: Marian Yule
memes.description: The novel's most sympathetic character — the intelligent, loyal daughter of Alfred Yule who works as a literary researcher and whose marriage prospects are determined by an inheritance she does not receive.

memes.label: 'Marylebone'
memes.description: "A London district that provides the setting for key social encounters in New Grub Street, associated with the more prosperous characters."

memes.label: 'Maud Milvain'
memes.description: "Jasper Milvain's younger sister, who shares his pragmatic approach to literary success and navigates the social world of Grub Street alongside her sister Dora."

memes.label: Miss Harrow
memes.description: A family friend of the Milvains with connections to Edmund Yule in George Gissing's New Grub Street (1891).

memes.label: Miss Rupert
memes.description: A wealthy young woman courted by Jasper Milvain for her fortune in George Gissing's New Grub Street (1891).

memes.label: Money in Literature
memes.description: The omnipresent economic calculus of literary life in New Grub Street — where every page is measured in pounds, every decision is shaped by the need for income, and the question of what a writer is worth pervades every relationship and plot development.

memes.label: 'Mornington Road'
memes.description: "A London street where characters in New Grub Street lodge, emblematic of the shifting fortunes of literary aspirants."

memes.label: 'Mr Bailey'
memes.description: 'A figure active in the London literary circles of New Grub Street, reflecting the interconnected nature of the Grub Street professional network.'

memes.label: Mr Bailey, Grocer
memes.description: Harold Biffen's realistic novel about a grocer, representing the ideal of uncompromising literary realism in New Grub Street.

memes.label: 'Mrs Edmund Yule'
memes.description: 'The wife of Edmund Yule and daughter-in-law of John Yule, navigating the complex family dynamics and inheritance expectations in New Grub Street.'

memes.label: Mudie's Library
memes.description: Mudie's Select Library, the dominant Victorian circulating library that shaped the three-volume novel system and literary marketplace depicted in New Grub Street.

memes.label: New Grub Street
memes.description: George Gissing's 1891 novel depicting the literary marketplace of late Victorian London — a stark portrait of writers struggling between artistic integrity and commercial success.

memes.label: "On Neutral Ground"
memes.description: "Edwin Reardon's commercially unsuccessful novel, representing the tension between artistic integrity and market demands."

memes.label: 'Oxford'
memes.description: "Referenced as a contrasting intellectual milieu to London's commercial literary world in New Grub Street."

memes.label: "Paternoster Row"
memes.description: "The historic centre of London publishing, symbolising the commercial heart of the literary trade."

memes.label: Publishing Trade
memes.description: The commercial infrastructure of literary production in New Grub Street — publishing houses, circulating libraries, and booksellers that mediate between writers and readers, shaping what gets written by controlling what gets sold.

memes.label: Putney
memes.description: A district in southwest London on the south bank of the Thames where Harold Biffen ends his life on Putney Heath; a site of tragic closure in the novel's final chapters.

memes.label: 'Quarmby'
memes.description: 'A veteran literary man, inveterate chatterbox and acquaintance of the Milvain circle, representing the older generation of Grub Street professionals.'

memes.label: "Regent's Park"
memes.description: "A key London setting in New Grub Street where characters walk, converse, and contemplate their literary ambitions."

memes.label: Reviewing and Criticism
memes.description: The practice of literary criticism and reviewing in the late-Victorian periodical press — a source of income for Grub Street writers, a gatekeeping function for the publishing industry, and a site of the novel's most cynical observations about literary culture.

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: The pervasive but often unspoken determinant of literary life in New Grub Street — where class position shapes access to education, marriage prospects, and even what kind of writing a person can afford to pursue.

memes.label: "St Paul's Crescent"
memes.description: A residential crescent in London where the Milvain sisters live; a modest but respectable address that serves as a social hub for the novel's literary characters.

memes.label: The Current
memes.description: A literary periodical edited by Clement Fadge that represents the competitive world of Grub Street journalism — where Jasper Milvain places his work and where the novel's characters jostle for position, publication, and payment.

memes.label: The Optimist
memes.description: Edwin Reardon's second novel, a commercial failure that precipitates his financial and psychological decline in New Grub Street.

memes.label: 'The Strand'
memes.description: "A major London thoroughfare that serves as a backdrop for the novel's literary commerce, connecting the worlds of publishing and daily life."

memes.label: "The Study"
memes.description: "A literary periodical edited by Fadge, central to the depiction of the periodical marketplace."

memes.label: "The Wayside"
memes.description: "A periodical referenced in New Grub Street, part of the competitive literary marketplace."

memes.label: "The West End"
memes.description: "A periodical mentioned in New Grub Street, representing the fashionable side of literary publishing."

memes.label: Three-Volume System
memes.description: The dominant publishing format for Victorian novels — a commercial constraint that shaped both literary production and the economic realities of the characters in New Grub Street.

memes.label: 'Tottenham Court Road'
memes.description: 'A London thoroughfare connecting different social spheres in the novel, mentioned as characters navigate the literary marketplace.'

memes.label: 'Wattleborough'
memes.description: "A provincial town in New Grub Street where the Yule family has connections."

memes.label: Westbourne Park
memes.description: A residential area in West London where Marian Yule lives with her mother; the destination of numerous visits by Jasper Milvain and others in the Yule family orbit.

memes.label: Whelpdale
memes.description: The novel's comic relief and unexpected success story — a hapless literary entrepreneur whose schemes, failures, and eventual marriage to Dora Milvain provide the novel's most optimistic resolution to the Grub Street dilemma.

memes.label: Wimbledon
memes.description: A southwestern suburb of London, home of Horace Barlow, where several key social encounters in New Grub Street take place.
